action.reasoning: Observation: All previous training attempts (including SimpleMLP on preprocessed features) crash with segfault or std::bad_alloc. Only test_env (128 graphs) succeeded, suggesting a memory corruption or leak after many batches. Analysis: The crash may originate from torch_geometric imports in model.py, which allocate CUDA extensions or cause memory fragmentation even when GNN layers are not used. By isolating to a pure PyTorch LinearMLP model (no torch_geometric) and forcing CPU, we eliminate potential sources of instability. Additionally, we use smaller hidden_dim=64, batch_size=32, and only 5 epochs to minimize memory pressure and get a baseline metric. Decision: Create model_linear.py, train_torch.py, and update predict.py to use the new model. Execute 5-epoch training on CPU. If successful, submit eval to finally obtain a baseline AP and measure per-epoch time.
